title: Virginia Man Makes 3 Pizzas in 31 Seconds Flat
script: 31 seconds. Three pizzas? Richard Delcid is a general manager at Domino's in Manassas, Virginia. Started there in 2012 as a part-time door hanger handing out coupons. Worked his way up to running the whole store. This spring, Domino's flew him to Las Vegas for the World's Fastest Pizza Maker competition — a real event the company has held since 1982. Finalists had to make 3 large pizzas — pepperoni, mushroom, and cheese — and pass a quality inspection: dough stretched right, sauce spread evenly, toppings distributed. And be the fastest. Delcid did it in 31.22 seconds, his third year competing and his first win. Walked away with $5,000, a championship belt, and a trophy. How is a 10-minute delivery even possible when the pizza only takes 31 seconds to make?
